text.title: Health Checks and Auto-Heal: Examples
text.summary: NERO platform watcher configuration with three service types (HTTP, Celery inspect, systemd), RabbitMQ management API health check, and Telegram alert integration.

example.code: SERVICES = [ ServiceCheck( "nero-core", check_celery("/srv/nero/nero-core/.venv", "nero_core"), "systemctl --user restart nero-core", ), ServiceCheck( "nero-channel-web", check_http("http://127.0.0.1:8100/health"), "systemctl --user restart nero-channel-web", ), ServiceCheck( "nero-channel-tg", check_systemd("nero-channel-tg"), "systemctl --user restart nero-channel-tg", ), ]

# Node health curl -su guest:guest http://localhost:15672/api/healthchecks/node
# Queue depth (alert if > 100 messages backed up) depth=$(curl -su guest:guest http://localhost:15672/api/queues/%2F/celery | jq '.messages') [ "$depth" -gt 100 ] && echo "WARNING: celery queue depth $depth"

#!/usr/bin/env bash # alert.sh — Send alert via Telegram bot MESSAGE="$1" HOSTNAME=$(hostname) TIMESTAMP=$(date -u +'%Y-%m-%d %H:%M UTC')
curl -s -X POST \ "https://api.telegram.org/bot${TELEGRAM_ALERT_BOT_TOKEN}/sendMessage" \ -d "chat_id=${TELEGRAM_ALERT_CHAT_ID}" \ -d "text=[${HOSTNAME}] ${TIMESTAMP}%0A${MESSAGE}" >/dev/null

see-also.link: Poll all services and print summary table
